codeReview.summary: The feature adds broad integration coverage, but several assertions are too weak to verify required behaviors in the feature contract.

issues.description: Hash-gating test only checks `unenriched_after > 0`, which does not validate the requirement that only changed nodes are marked for re-enrichment; it can pass even if unchanged nodes are also cleared.
DB path consistency test covers init/build/status/query/reset but omits enrich, while the feature requires DB path consistency across all commands.
Cross-language edge validation asserts only `edges > 0`, which does not prove edges exist across language boundaries; same-language edges would also satisfy this.
Test helper hardcodes `/Users/kjackowski/.factory/bin` into PATH, reducing portability across environments.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-cli-worker skill requires explicit manual verification (step 6), but the transcript skeleton for this session shows only automated test commands before commit while handoff marks `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 3520e7af-29dd-4953-8f93-2bc076c0d69a shows pytest runs and commit flow without separate manual CLI verification commands; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no explicit shared convention documenting that tests should avoid machine-specific absolute paths; this allowed a hardcoded PATH entry in integration tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/test_integration.py:25 hardcodes `/Users/kjackowski/.factory/bin`; AGENTS.md and .factory/library docs do not define a portability rule for test-time PATH handling.

summary: Reviewed feature cross-area-integration for commit 75e98ef using handoff, transcript skeleton, diff, and shared-state docs. Result is fail due to three blocking coverage-quality gaps: hash-gating specificity, missing enrich in DB-path consistency checks, and insufficient proof of cross-language edges.
